title: Today's Markets Brief: US charges Google engineer with insider trading on Polymarket
summary: A look at the latest finance news, including US charges against a Google engineer and oil price jumps.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's markets brief. I've got my coffee in hand, and I'm ready to walk you through the latest headlines in the world of finance. Let's dive right in.

First up, we've got a story that's got everyone talking. The US has charged a Google engineer with insider trading on Polymarket. Now, I know some of you might be thinking, 'Polymarket, what's that?' Well, it's a decentralized prediction market platform that allows users to trade on the outcome of various events. And it looks like one Google engineer might have gotten a little too close to the action. According to reports, the engineer allegedly used confidential information to make trades on the platform. It's a reminder that even in the world of crypto and decentralized finance, regulators are still watching closely for any signs of misconduct.

Next, let's talk about oil prices. They jumped on Thursday after fresh US strikes in Iran renewed concerns about disruptions to commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Now, this is a region that's always been a bit of a flashpoint for global tensions, and it's no surprise that oil prices are sensitive to any signs of instability. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical waterway for oil exports, and any disruption to shipping could have a big impact on global supplies. So, it's not hard to see why traders are getting a little nervous.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
